programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: The crew of Essex and Herts Air Ambulance race to help teenager Jack who has a major brain injury after colliding with a car in Hemel Hempstead.

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours in...
programme.desc: Joe Lycett sets off on more mini-breaks. Sarah Millican joins Joe in the Lithuanian capital Vilnius, where hot air ballooning, cepelini dumplings and gira, and the Lukiskes Prison await.

programme.title: Jamies One-Pan Wonders
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver makes a sublime dukkah roast lamb feast with a fantastic hack to speed up cooking.

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George helps out at the restoration of an Edwardian arts and crafts terrace house in south London, where the amazing original features need plenty of TLC.

programme.title: Matt Baker: Travels with Mum and Dad
programme.desc: In Northumberland, the Bakers explore Lord William Armstrongs pioneering hydro-electric inventions at Cragside House and Gardens and Cragend Farm.

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: In Andalucía, Michael and Shaun host their first wedding at their retreat. In Italy, Jane and Walter face difficult decisions over their unique BandBs future.

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: The Garners tug on Alan and Tinas heartstrings, but can they loosen their purse strings? The Aldertons get motorcycle-mad Keith and Margaret revved up with characterful villas.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Joy and John from Chesterfield are searching for their dream holiday home around Fuengirola on Spains Costa del Sol. Ben Hillman is on hand with five great properties lined up.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Teacher Wendys looking for her dream holiday home in Puglia, southern Italy. Jean Johannson lines up five great properties for her £200k, but will any of them get top marks?

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: Ben and Billie battle brambles to create a riverside retreat. Louisa and Rogers new suite has last-minute design changes. Mark and Sandra scramble to ready their gîte for guests.

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It: Brilliant Builds
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer take a look back on two homes which needed storage solutions and savvy spending, from shilly-shallying in Stoke to disorganised chaos in Newcastle.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: First to host is 69-year-old former property developer, Ian. Ians hoping to impress his guests with his super-healthy menu, but its possible they may be more impressed with his 12-bedroom mansion in the country.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the turn of 24-year-old reigning Miss Cheltenham, Sophie to host. Sophie is hoping to impress her guests with an evening of elegant food and pageantry.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the third day of the competition in Gloucestershire and the turn of 27-year-old rugby-loving Carmel to host; shes confident that her fun filled evening will kick the competition into touch.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the turn of 40-year-old school teacher Polly to host. Polly is hoping to wow her guests with a fine dining experience consisting of a seafood starter and a meaty main, undeterred by the fact that she has never cooked either dish before.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the final day of the competition in Gloucestershire, and the turn of exuberant hair salon owner Chris to host. Chris is hosting a high-octane evening with some special homemade treats on the menu.

programme.title: Secret Africa: Into The Wild
programme.desc: Explorer Lucy Shepherd embarks on an epic journey across Tanzania with the nomadic Indigenous Hadza and Akie tribesmen. With no crew and no safety net, survival over the next six weeks depends on working with the wild rather than against it.

programme.title: Cruising to the Ends of the Earth
programme.desc: An unforgettable voyage to the worlds most awe-inspiring destinations. From Alaskas glacial ice fields to Japan and the sun-drenched Sicilian port of Messina, we see what cruise ship life is really like.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Dad-to-be Josh, whos 27, has come off his motorbike at speed. Georgie brings in her 10-month-old son Phoenix, whos been sucking on a bleach tablet.

programme.title: Super Surgeons: A Chance at Life
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the world-leading Royal Marsden Hospital, as surgeons attempt pioneering cancer treatments, including a high-risk bid to save a womans voice box.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britains sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the weeks biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Some of Britains best loved famous faces share their sharp, insightful, funny and emotional views on popular and topical TV shows. From Saturday night entertainment juggernauts to the weeks biggest news stories, celebs become opinionated viewers.

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Confident soldier Paul is the opposite of what ex girl band member Natalie goes for. But will she fall for his charm? And can weightlifting octogenarian Dave woo the elegant Clare?

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Luke, whos lost his dating mojo, is raring to reinvigorate his love life and find a lady to relight his fire. And can pole dancer Abi find someone to send her heart into a spin?

programme.title: Open House: The Great Sex Experiment
programme.desc: Katey and Dan plan a night of fun with residents Josh and Holly, while Nicolle and Ben take the next step into non-monogamy and try a couple swap. Can Nicolle overcome her anxiety and insecurities?

programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.title: Traffic Cops

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A look back at the highlights of the series, including the units efforts to catch a stolen 4x4 that tries to run a police Evo off the road. Elsewhere, officers have a trick up their sleeve when a speeding driver attempts to outrun nine cop cars.

programme.title: The Deadly Companions
programme.desc: Soon after the Civil War, a Yankee officer arrives in town and saves a card cheat from a lynching. Their intention of robbing the poorly guarded bank is thwarted when masked raiders beat them to it and in an ensuing gun-fight, the young son of a cabaret dancer is accidentally shot dead. The remorseful officer insists on escorting the dancer with the body when she decides to bury her son in Apache territory.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One After the Super Bowl
programme.desc: Ross finally spends some time with Marcel after many false starts, and Chandler is delighted to get a date with the sexy Susie, who he hasnt seen since fourth grade. However, she gets her revenge for youthful humiliation by leaving him in the loo, naked but for a pair of her knickers.

programme.title: Friends

programme.title: The World Is A Wonder

programme.title: Wallace and Gromit in The Curse of the Were-Rabbit
programme.desc: The village is being overrun by rabbits and Wallace has a plan to alter their minds so that they will leave the vegetable allotments alone. However, his plan goes awry and the rabbit problem escalates when the villagers discover a monstrous `were-rabbit destroying everything in its sights. Will Wallace and Gromit be able to solve the conundrum before the creature gets shot?

programme.title: Lon le Linda
programme.desc: Linda and her friends make a colourful and tasty salad.

programme.title: Oscar and Ealasaid
programme.desc: As soon as it freezes, the bears go into hibernation, except for Ernest, who claims hes accustomed to extreme temperatures. When Celestine goes to get some flour from her friends, a blizzard begins to blow.

programme.title: Cleasan Ataidh and Tilidh
programme.desc: Abney plants seeds in the moonlight. In the morning some have grown into lovely big cabbages. Abney and Teal decide to help out one that is struggling, which leads to big trouble.

programme.title: Abadas
programme.desc: The Abadas are pirates, hunting for treasure. One lucky pirate is chosen to go in search of an anchor.

programme.title: An Teaghlach Rìoghail an Ath-dhoras
programme.desc: Tha mòr-amharas ann nuair a tha an Rìgh a nochdadh gun a chrùn air aig ball-coise. Theres much suspicion when the King appears at football without his crown.

programme.title: An Teaghlach Rìoghail an Ath-dhoras
programme.desc: Tha Stella maslaichte mas fhaic a caraidean i aig picnic teaghlaich sa phàirc. Stella is embarrassed in case friends see her at a family picnic in the park.

programme.title: Uaine
programme.desc: Were not short of water in Scotland and Calum is in the Highlands to find out more about hydroelectric power .

programme.title: Clann Na Cruinne
programme.desc: Grga has to earn some cash so he can fulfill his dream - flying in a hot air balloon.

programme.title: An La

programme.title: An Clo Mor
programme.desc: The arrival of potential Norwegian investor Johan throws a cat among the pigeons as age old family secrets and resentments come to the fore.

programme.title: Prosbaig
programme.desc: Donald Morrison takes a look back at the uncertainty surrounding the completion of the Rhenigidale Road midway through construction, the people of Berneray campaigning for a causeway, and fears that the prosperity the klondyker boom brought to Ullapool was nearing an end.

programme.title: Immigration Tracks
programme.desc: Anne meets the Rubilar family in Montreal. Originally from Chile, they are now searching for their great grandfather, Daniel Morrison from the Isle of Lewis.

This episode features performances from Ciorstaidh Beaton, Eric Bibb, Loudon Wainwright III, Kíla and Joshua Burnside. In Gaelic with English subtitles.

programme.title: Third Lanark
programme.desc: In 1967, as it edged towards its centenary, a much-loved social and cultural institution was consigned to history. Following 95 years of emotional highs and lows, Glasgows Third Lanark Athletic Club was bankrupt, and the gates to Cathkin Park were closed.

programme.title: Sgeulachd Cogaidh na h-Artaig
programme.desc: Discover why the desire to capture Nazi weapon technology turns the U.S. and USSR from WW II allies into Cold War enemies; look at how the superpowers try to gain an Arctic foothold, from guerrilla warfare to intelligence schemes to nuclear arms.

programme.title: Smoggie Queens

programme.title: The Beauty Queen and the Catfish
programme.desc: When a mysterious doctor starts flirting with a former beauty queen online, she smells a love rat. But when she starts digging, the mystery deepens, and nothing is as it seems.

programme.title: The Beauty Queen and the Catfish
programme.desc: The women work together to reveal the true identity of their catfish. However, as more victims and evidence come to light, the women are forced to take matters into their own hands.

programme.title: The Beauty Queen and the Catfish
programme.desc: With the perpetrator now investigated by the police as a serial offender, questions turn to what drove them to become one of the UKs prolific catfish - and why cant they stop?

programme.title: Stacey Dooley

programme.desc: Grizzy and the Lemmings get into a fight over the last jar of chocolate spread in the cabin and it ends up sinking to the bottom of the lagoon.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: SpongeBob and Patrick help Bubble Bass move out of his mothers basement.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck and Frog must move a famous pop stars possessions from one mansion to another.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck is working overnight in a museum and creates a Frankenstein Frog to keep him company.

programme.title: Odd Squad

programme.title: Bitesize: 7-9 Year Olds

programme.title: One Zoo Three

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: Take a journey through one of the weirdest, wildest periods in English history, featuring the only time that a king has been tried and executed for treason!

programme.title: Blow Your Mind: Incredible Icebergs
programme.desc: Chris van Tulleken, Xand van Tulleken. Dr Chris and Dr Xand van Tulleken learn all about ice. They follow a group of top scientists and explorers to find out how icebergs are born and what happens to them before they melt and disappear. They arrive at Store Glacier in Greenland, a gigantic 400m kilometre river of ice that releases 15 billion tonnes of ice into the sea each year as icebergs.

programme.title: Art Ninja
programme.desc: Ricky decides to get in shape and makes healthy art, including fruit pom-poms and a salad-face place mat. Ricky tries to sign up for some ninja fitness classes, but ends up making some revolutionary art and facing a bit of a high jump.

programme.title: Hey You What If?
programme.desc: If the beach is too far to go but you still want to spend some time surfing waves, then why not surf on land instead? Well, there are some very good reasons people dont surf on land. To surf, you need waves which are steep enough that you can literally slide down them - thats how surfing works. You slide down the face whilst the wave continuously picks you up so you can just keep sliding. Finding waves the right shape on land is quite a challenge.

programme.title: Deadly Dinosaurs with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve goes on a mission to uncover what was deadlier than the dinosaurs - from massive monsoons and supersized sandstorms to ballistic bacteria and gory gas.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing and Sula are building a stone tower in the Howly Woods when Sula finds an ammonite. Bing wants to find one too but is overwhelmed by the amount of stones, which makes it very difficult to find one. A frustrated Bing sits down, and Sula notices some ammonites in the rock behind him. Flop takes a picture of Bing and Sula with their ammonites.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Mojo and Bo only wanted to help fix a satellite dish with Mojos Chopper Top. Now theyve been mistaken for a UFO, and the whole town is in a tizzy about invading aliens!

programme.desc: The Squirrels get in line to have their photo taken. Say cheese Squirrels!

programme.desc: The squirrels wonder what Duggee is up to. He seems to be looking through a long stick out of the window. Whats that, Duggee? Duggee explains that its a telescope, and hes looking at things far, far away in space!

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Mum and Dad vow to only finish their boring housework when the kids stop acting silly, but thats never going to happen, so they end up trying the same approach themselves.

programme.desc: When Muffin cant stop sucking her thumb, she has to wear a cone of shame. But the cone gets in her way and she can no longer play `sandwich shop with Bluey and Bingo.

programme.desc: Bing and Pando discover a new climbing frame at the playground. It has climbing bits, tippy bits and even a wobbly bridge. But when Bing gets to the top of the slide its too scary for him to go down. Bing watches Pando to see how he does it, then he helps Hoppity Voosh to go down. When Hoppity likes it, Bing has a go too.

programme.desc: A celebration of communicating and encouraging children to talk with a focus on one word, showing various visualisations of a word in a rhythmical, bouncy, humorous way!

programme.desc: Gemma, Benjee Bear and Bailee Bear welcome toddlers Elena, Brodhi, Cavalli and Betsy, and their grown-ups, to the Toddler Club. Parents, carers and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too.

programme.title: I Spy, You Spy

programme.title: Whats in Your Plant Pot?
programme.desc: Food stylist Rob introduces the Fact Pack to edible flowers. They discover that some special flowers can be eaten, how different flowers taste, and how edible flowers can make food look and taste exciting.

programme.title: Monster Makes
programme.desc: Rebecca and Murphy are dealing with a glut of carrots from the vegetable patch, and they have made everything from carrot cake to carrot juice. This inspires Murphys first idea - a cartoon bunny rabbit. The Mini Monsters are making colourful caterpillars, and Rebeccas uncle is snug as a bug in his woolly cardigan when he calls her on the Magic Monster Mirror. The Recycling Express brings an empty box, which Rebecca and Murphy transform into a cardboard kitty cat.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Ginger and the Peas discover a more efficient way to explore the valley - by tunnel!

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Dad demonstrates to Bluey and Bingo just how difficult it is to look after a pet.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are crafting in the Clubhouse when Betty saves Tag from throwing water across the table instead of glitter. Tag, although full of enthusiasm and energy, has not always been the best listener. He was great at discovering new things, and everyone wanted to join in with him - but getting easily distracted would land him in an array of trouble.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Billy the bug here bringing some cheer for that special time of year, harvest time.

programme.title: My First Festivals
programme.desc: Following twins Art and Enda as they celebrate the Buddhist festival of Vesak. Art and Enda begin at the park, where they learn about the lotus flower from a Buddhist monk.

programme.title: Our Family
programme.sub-title: Iona and Elbas Mission to Mars
programme.desc: Iona and Elba make bird feeders as a present for their Nanny Pip and Gramps, who they are visiting. They place the feeders around the garden and wait patiently for birds to arrive, hoping theyll discover the tasty treats.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Ryans Vietnamese Pork and Prawn Cha
programme.desc: Ryans mum is Vietnamese and he is cooking a dish from Vietnam that she has taught him. He works hard in the kitchen mixing lots of ingredients together, including pork, prawns and fresh mint. He even finds time to teach us the Vietnamese word for egg! One of Ryans ingredients is fresh mint, but not all of Ryans friends like the taste or smell of it at first.

programme.title: Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie finds out about things that go up and down. She shows how a lift works when it carries people up and down between floors of a building. With the help of an engineer and her special cameras, Maddie show how the gears and weights are controlled by a computer. Next, its time for lift-off as Maddie heads to a space museum.

programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: Dan the border collie is a search and rescue dog who works with his owner Jamie. His super nose follows scents and smells like clues, so they can rescue people who need their help. A mountain biker called Eddie hasnt made it back from a ride with his friends, and its starting to get dark. Police officer Penny needs Jamie and Dans help with a night search. They head to where he was last seen, and a friendly owl helps Dan find his scent.

programme.title: Aneeshwar and Lizzie Go Wild
programme.desc: To be advised.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Following a rocky start in Red River Gorge, Barneys got another activity in store - a paranormal one, at the haunted Poasttown Elementary School. The next day, the Walshes wake up in Louisville, the home of Muhammad Ali. There they follow in the footsteps of `The Champ, sparring down at a local boxing ring to work up an appetite. Barney rewards his dad with a well-earned KFC.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: In Ohio, Brad and Barney head straight for Dayton, the home of aviation. After recreating history in the Wright B Flyer, its Barneys turn to take the wheel - of a plane. The Walshes attend a local rodeo, and Brads quick to realise theyre not just there to spectate: theyll be taking part in `the most dangerous eight seconds in sport. However, after a nasty fall from an angry bull, Brad ends up spending the night in hospital, giving `Breaking Dad a whole new meaning.

programme.desc: Frank offers to paint Rays house but refuses to paint it the colour that Ray and Debra want.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: As Ray escorts Ally to a father-daughter dance, he realises how grown up and independent his daughter has become, and recalls the day when she was born.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray and Debra are taken aback when Michael shares a story about a bickering family at his schools open house.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Frank is so envious that he forces Marco to choose either Marie or him as a friend, and Ray sides with Frank because he doesnt approve of a married woman, especially his mother, having a male friend.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Rosss Library Book
programme.desc: Ross is elated that the university library contains his doctoral dissertation, but he is shocked to discover that it is located in a remote corner frequently used by students having sex. Elsewhere, the tables are turned on Joey when Erin is the one to just want sex and not a relationship.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Chandler Doesnt Like Dogs
programme.desc: Joey may have ruined Rachels chances with Tag when he opens his mouth during Thanksgiving dinner, and Phoebe reveals that she has been secretly keeping a cute dog in her room for a few days while its owner is away.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With All the Candy
programme.desc: Rachel is dating her assistant, Tag, but they must keep it a secret at work because of the companys anti-dating policy. Her friends warn her that the relationship will only cause her trouble, but just for fun, she becomes more brazen about it.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Holiday Armadillo
programme.desc: Ross unexpectedly finds himself looking after Ben and decides to do something special for Christmas. Since all the Santa costumes have been rented out, Ross dons the best outfit left and entertains Ben as the holiday armadillo.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With All the Cheesecakes
programme.desc: Chandler and Rachel pig out on a neighbours cheesecake that was mistakenly delivered to Chandler. They feel bad, but not quite guilty enough to refrain from eating a second one that has also been wrongly delivered.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Theyre Up All Night
programme.desc: During their fist night together, Rachel and Tag realise they forgot to ship out an important package at the office.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebes new job as a telemarketer gets off to a tricky start when the first person she calls, an office worker called Earl, tells her he feels unappreciated and wants to kill himself. Phoebe keeps him on the telephone, trying to persuade him otherwise and finally takes the desperate measure of going to his office to convince him to stay alive.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where They All Turn Thirty
programme.desc: Celebrating Rachels 30th birthday inspires the friends to reminisce about their own 30th birthdays. Joey got angry with God for breaking their deal, Ross bought himself a sports car in an attempt to recapture his youth, and Monica got blind drunk and embarrassed herself at her surprise party.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Joeys New Brain
programme.desc: Joey accidentally informs a cast member that she will be fired so that his character will get her characters brain, and Ross plans a surprise for Monica and Chandlers wedding - he wants to play the bagpipes.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Truth About London
programme.desc: Monica and Chandler fondly reminisce about London until a startling revelation leaves Chandler floored. Meanwhile, Rachel teaches Ben practical jokes to play on Ross.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Cheap Wedding Dress
programme.desc: Monica regrets telling a fellow bride-to-be about a cheaper bridal shop, and later she has to fight the woman tooth and nail for a unique wedding dress before competing with her to hire Chandlers favourite band for the night. Meanwhile, Joey and Ross discover that theyre dating the same girl.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey is overjoyed to be nominated for a `Soapie, the third most prestigious award in daytime dramas. Meanwhile, engaged Monica frets when she realises she will never experience the thrill of a new relationship again, Ross spots Chandler sneaking out of a male strip club, and Phoebe meets a cute guy.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Ross and Monicas Cousin
programme.desc: Monica is so perturbed by Chandlers instant attraction to her gorgeous visiting cousin that she persuades Ross to let her stay at his apartment. Despite being her blood relative, Ross cannot restrain himself from attempting to kiss her. She escapes to Phoebes apartment, but her beauty flabbergasts even Phoebe.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Rachels Big Kiss

programme.title: Friends

programme.title: Friends

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Airing from 2005 to 2009 on NBC, the game show series returns to primetime TV with original host Howie Mandel. A contestant must choose from 26 sealed briefcases containing a marker for various amounts of cash -- from one penny to $1 million. The player then eliminates the remaining 25 cases one by one. The chosen ones are opened and the amount of money inside revealed. After several cases are opened, the player is tempted by the Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for not continuing the game and possibly winning a larger sum of money.

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Five million-dollar cases are on the board as a woman seven months pregnant plays to win cash.

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: A contestant receives a NASCAR package presented by race car driver Jimmy Johnson, and must choose between a lifelong dream and a selfless act for his family.

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: A physical education teacher and avid video game player is surprised with extra $1 million cases on the board and the chance to be immortalized in a video game; a woman from Chicago plays with extra $1 million cases on the board.

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: A physical education teacher and avid video game player is surprised with extra $1 million cases on the board and the chance to be immortalized in a video game; a woman from Chicago plays with extra $1 million cases on the board.

programme.title: Gold Divers
programme.desc: In the fleets last push for gold, Reaper Nation fights to mine until the cold, bitter end. Shawn reaches for gold in deeper waters. Emily must depend on her last remaining diver.

programme.title: Moonshiners
programme.desc: Mark and Digger find a solution to the jar shortage but the juice may not be worth the squeeze. Tim enlists Tickles friend Howard to help recreate the first whiskey made in America. Mike and Jerry distil vodka from surplus restaurant supplies.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: How Its Made
programme.desc: Paving asphalt, marshmallow cookies, loudspeakers, and electronic door locks. Where did they come from and how were they made?

programme.title: How Its Made
programme.sub-title: Wood Burning Stoves, Orthoses, Ballet Slippers, Buses
programme.desc: Wood-burning stoves, ballet slippers and buses: more everyday items are put under the microscope to find out how they were made.

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: The Mob Museum makes Wayde and Brett an offer they simply cannot refuse. They want a tank with replica sunken rumrunner for their prohibition exhibit and speakeasy in downtown Las Vegas.

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: A company designing customisations for high-end and rare vehicles want an exotic fish tank. They want a huge, three-sided build as their centrepiece and the team have a challenge on their hands.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Parkers Trail
programme.desc: An encounter with a group of machete-wielding locals tests the teams preparedness for the regions dangerous conditions after a gruelling jungle trek leads Parker and his team to a network of abandoned WWII tunnels.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Parkers Trail
programme.desc: A sudden landslide rips Parker off his feet while hes working on a local hard rock mining operation. Dozer Dave bets his life savings that he can unearth piles of gold using salvaged equipment and a team of amateurs.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid: Yurts So Good
programme.desc: Chris and Caitlin Williams are building the home of their dreams: a 30-foot tall, 700-square-foot, off-grid yurt in Montana, complete with a breathtaking view of the mountains, solar power, its own stream and hot running water.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid
programme.desc: Furniture DIY-ers Ana and Jacob White build their dream cabin in the remote Alaskan interior on Paxson Lake, battling extreme elements while putting their carpentry skills to the ultimate test.

programme.title: Dr. Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Tigger the cat is monitored closely for unusual eating habits. Plus, Dr. Jeff cautions an owner of the perils of buying online when she brings in her unwell puppy.

programme.title: Dr. Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: The team lend a hand at Lakota Reservation in South Dakota. Back at the clinic, Amy debates emergency surgery for a French bulldog who ate his favourite toy.

programme.sub-title: NASCAR Car Bodies; Hurley Sticks; Tube Power Amplifiers; Thermal Coffee Pots
programme.desc: NASCAR car bodies; hurley sticks; tube power amplifiers; thermal coffee pots.

programme.desc: Electric vehicle charging stations; grappa; lunar rover replicas.

programme.desc: Racing leathers; evaporative cooling towers; wood rocking chairs; wire wheels.

programme.title: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: The Raneys help an Oregon couple with a toxic salt water well. Marty builds a massive catchment pond and Matt works on a solar still to decontaminate the water.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: To celebrate the series 20th anniversary, Mike and Elvis set out to restore a Ford Fiesta Mark 1 and stage the shows largest ever car meet.

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.title: Expedition X
programme.desc: Reports of shadowy figures and malevolent spirits from Caribbean folklore draw Phil and Heather to Trinidad. They explore a notorious plantation and an abandoned island once used as a leper colony, searching for the source of the chilling activity.

programme.title: Conspiracies and Cover-Ups
programme.desc: Andrew probes COVID-19s mysterious origin from all angles. Using intelligence tradecraft, expert interviews, and real-world analysis, he investigates natural spillover, lab-related, and accidental release theories.

programme.title: Expedition Bigfoot
programme.desc: The teams investigations converge as Bryce, Mireya, and Russell discover tree structures on different sides of the same mountain. As a storm approaches, Bryce prepares an AI-powered surveillance array that will help pinpoint Bigfoots location.

programme.title: Border Control: Spain
programme.desc: At the border crossing La Línea, near Gibraltar, a nervous-looking man is stopped. The customs officers ask if they can view his trunk.

programme.title: Border Control: Spain
programme.desc: At Adolfo Suárez Airport, Madrid-Barajas, border guards see two unusual suitcases when checking airline passengers luggage. Customs specialists can already see from the X-ray that the luggage is incorrect.

programme.title: Expedition X
programme.desc: Reports of shadowy figures and malevolent spirits from Caribbean folklore draw Phil and Heather to Trinidad. They explore a notorious plantation and an abandoned island once used as a leper colony, searching for the source of the chilling activity.

programme.title: Moonshiners
programme.desc: Mark and Digger invent a new recipe turning hundreds of jars of expired mandarin oranges into marketable liquor. Tickle and the Laws face headaches in their attempt to produce freeze-distilled apple liquor. Mike and Jerry make birch bark moonshine.

programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: Experts answer questions about surprising animal behaviour including how a squirrel takes on a dangerous rattle snake, what makes eagles eyes so powerful, and why the planet is overrun with bugs.

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: Experts answer questions about surprising animal behaviour, including whether parrots really know what theyre talking about, how electric eels manage not to electrocute themselves, and why pandas are black and white.

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: New science and amazing demonstrations explain the bizarre, the brilliant and the most mind-blowing animal behaviours.

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: A look at why spiders dont get stuck in their own webs, and how foxes find water in the desert.

programme.title: Dr. Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: In one of the busiest clinics in America, Dr Jeff and his team are faced with saving a dog that goes into cardiac arrest shortly after arriving at his clinic.

programme.title: Dr. Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: A dog is brought into the clinic with a suspected broken leg, but Dr Jeff quickly diagnoses a far more serious condition. Plus, a cats appetite gets it in trouble.

programme.title: Dr. Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: A micro-pig with an injured leg requires surgery, but anaesthesia could be deadly. Later, a French bulldogs routine dental exam becomes a complex operation.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Mine Rescue With Freddy and Juan
programme.desc: In the Yukon, Freddy and Juan tackle their most extensive treasure hunt yet: 288 claims across 6,000 acres and double the breakdowns. As the seconds tick away on the miners season, the duo teams up with a local expert to find the gold.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Mine Rescue With Freddy and Juan
programme.desc: Hoping to right the mining mistakes of his father, a rookie Idaho miner closes his failing 30-year-old auto business to chase gold in the remote Idaho Hills.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Mine Rescue With Freddy and Juan
programme.desc: In North Americas most extreme goldfields, thousands of unprepared miners gamble it all, chasing the highest gold prices in history -- and find themselves on the brink of ruin. Their last hope: Freddy and Juan.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Mine Rescue With Freddy and Juan
programme.desc: In California, a miner with 75 cents in the bank faces ruin at his failing hard-rock mine; when a pivot to placer mining fails, Freddy makes an incredible discovery, and Juan builds their first-ever hard-rock mill hammer system to save this dad.

programme.title: Filthy Fortunes
programme.desc: Matt scrambles to clear a bat-infested barn and turn a sea of Santa Clauses into big money. While tackling jobs in Michigan and Massachusetts, Matt finds a lost cache of rare coins.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Chase Morrill and his team renovate an off-the-grid hunting cabin deep in the woods of Oxford, ME. They have a budget of $20,000 and a time frame of six weeks, but can they get the cabin done before winter comes?

programme.sub-title: This Is Gonna Suck So Bad
programme.desc: From Mexicos Yucatán to Botswanas Okavango Delta, fan favourites Ernie and Mandy return -- this time for 21 days. Armed with new tools and improved skills, can they survive? Or will Africa eat them alive?

programme.desc: Dark matter is one of the greatest mysteries of the universe facing scientists today. Could it be the cause of supermassive black holes and mass extinctions?

programme.title: How the Universe Works

programme.desc: Reports of shadowy figures and malevolent spirits from Caribbean folklore draw Phil and Heather to Trinidad. They explore a notorious plantation and an abandoned island once used as a leper colony, searching for the source of the chilling activity.

programme.title: Conspiracies and Cover-Ups
programme.desc: Andrew probes COVID-19s mysterious origin from all angles. Using intelligence tradecraft, expert interviews, and real-world analysis, he investigates natural spillover, lab-related, and accidental release theories.

programme.title: Expedition Bigfoot
programme.desc: The teams investigations converge as Bryce, Mireya, and Russell discover tree structures on different sides of the same mountain. As a storm approaches, Bryce prepares an AI-powered surveillance array that will help pinpoint Bigfoots location.

programme.title: Border Control: Spain
programme.desc: At the border crossing La Línea, near Gibraltar, a nervous-looking man is stopped. The customs officers ask if they can view his trunk.

programme.title: Border Control: Spain
programme.desc: At Adolfo Suárez Airport, Madrid-Barajas, border guards see two unusual suitcases when checking airline passengers luggage. Customs specialists can already see from the X-ray that the luggage is incorrect.

programme.title: Expedition X
programme.desc: Reports of shadowy figures and malevolent spirits from Caribbean folklore draw Phil and Heather to Trinidad. They explore a notorious plantation and an abandoned island once used as a leper colony, searching for the source of the chilling activity.

programme.title: Conspiracies and Cover-Ups
programme.desc: Andrew probes COVID-19s mysterious origin from all angles. Using intelligence tradecraft, expert interviews, and real-world analysis, he investigates natural spillover, lab-related, and accidental release theories.

programme.title: Gold Divers
programme.desc: In a last push, Reaper Nation fights to mine until the cold, bitter end. Shawn reaches for gold in deeper waters. Emily must depend on her last remaining diver.

programme.title: Moonshiners
programme.desc: Mark and Digger invent a new recipe turning hundreds of jars of expired mandarin oranges into marketable liquor. Tickle and the Laws face headaches in their attempt to produce freeze-distilled apple liquor. Mike and Jerry make birch bark moonshine.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: Experts answer questions about surprising animal behaviour including how a squirrel takes on a dangerous rattle snake, what makes eagles eyes so powerful, and why the planet is overrun with bugs.

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: Experts answer questions about surprising animal behaviour, including whether parrots really know what theyre talking about, how electric eels manage not to electrocute themselves, and why pandas are black and white.

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: New science and amazing demonstrations explain the bizarre, the brilliant and the most mind-blowing animal behaviours.

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: A look at why spiders dont get stuck in their own webs, and how foxes find water in the desert.

programme.title: Dr. Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: In one of the busiest clinics in America, Dr Jeff and his team are faced with saving a dog that goes into cardiac arrest shortly after arriving at his clinic.

programme.title: Dr. Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: A dog is brought into the clinic with a suspected broken leg, but Dr Jeff quickly diagnoses a far more serious condition. Plus, a cats appetite gets it in trouble.

programme.title: Dr. Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: A micro-pig with an injured leg requires surgery, but anaesthesia could be deadly. Later, a French bulldogs routine dental exam becomes a complex operation.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Mine Rescue With Freddy and Juan
programme.desc: In the Yukon, Freddy and Juan tackle their most extensive treasure hunt yet: 288 claims across 6,000 acres and double the breakdowns. As the seconds tick away on the miners season, the duo teams up with a local expert to find the gold.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Mine Rescue With Freddy and Juan
programme.desc: Hoping to right the mining mistakes of his father, a rookie Idaho miner closes his failing 30-year-old auto business to chase gold in the remote Idaho Hills.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Mine Rescue With Freddy and Juan
programme.desc: In North Americas most extreme goldfields, thousands of unprepared miners gamble it all, chasing the highest gold prices in history -- and find themselves on the brink of ruin. Their last hope: Freddy and Juan.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Mine Rescue With Freddy and Juan
programme.desc: In California, a miner with 75 cents in the bank faces ruin at his failing hard-rock mine; when a pivot to placer mining fails, Freddy makes an incredible discovery, and Juan builds their first-ever hard-rock mill hammer system to save this dad.

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.title: Filthy Fortunes
programme.desc: Matt scrambles to clear a bat-infested barn and turn a sea of Santa Clauses into big money. While tackling jobs in Michigan and Massachusetts, Matt finds a lost cache of rare coins.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Chase Morrill and his team renovate an off-the-grid hunting cabin deep in the woods of Oxford, ME. They have a budget of $20,000 and a time frame of six weeks, but can they get the cabin done before winter comes?

programme.title: Naked and Afraid
programme.sub-title: This Is Gonna Suck So Bad
programme.desc: From Mexicos Yucatán to Botswanas Okavango Delta, fan favourites Ernie and Mandy return -- this time for 21 days. Armed with new tools and improved skills, can they survive? Or will Africa eat them alive?

programme.title: Expedition X
programme.desc: Reports of shadowy figures and malevolent spirits from Caribbean folklore draw Phil and Heather to Trinidad. They explore a notorious plantation and an abandoned island once used as a leper colony, searching for the source of the chilling activity.

programme.title: How the Universe Works
programme.desc: Dark matter is one of the greatest mysteries of the universe facing scientists today. Could it be the cause of supermassive black holes and mass extinctions?

programme.title: How the Universe Works

programme.title: Street Outlaws: Memphis
programme.desc: JJ agrees to take on a team from Arizona, but come race night the MSO realises their opponents have an arsenal of ridiculously expensive cars.

programme.title: Street Outlaws: Memphis
programme.desc: The streets of Las Vegas havent been kind to the MSO. Local racer Brett tries to take advantage by putting together a group of hand-picked ringers to whoop on JJ and his family.

programme.desc: Declassified video captured by the U.S. Navy triggers Jess and Phils investigation into mysterious, supersonic craft flying above -- and racing below -- the waters off Californias Catalina Island.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Club Med Europe Africa Middle East CEO Xavier Mufraggi goes undercover at his holiday parks.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling
programme.sub-title: Letting Go Is Hard to Do
programme.desc: With one kid already in college, Adina and Trey are looking to downsize. They are eager to sell fast before a subdivision floods the neighbourhood with a thousand brand new homes, but their outdated property is making a lousy first impression.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Oasis tribute singer Degsy and his wife Jackie kick things off with a Manchester-themed menu. Second night hosts Jo and Vikki serve an Indian-themed menu, and the final night sees Chris and Bex stepping up to the plate.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Newlyweds Jen and Callum kick off the competition in Aberdeen with an American-themed menu. Day two sees Debbie and Shaun opting for a Scottish themed menu, and finally its the turn of fun-loving couple Sam and Jamie to host.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One with the Drunk Bride
programme.desc: Nick and Laila are madly in love and have been engaged for two years. It would have taken them years to save up for Lailas dream wedding day, instead they will receive £12,000 towards their big day and a chance for Nick to prove himself to the woman he loves. They have agreed that Nick will organise the wedding alone and the bride wont know a single thing about it until the big day.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Tony and Pippa are desperate to get hitched, but dont have the cash. They are given £12,000 and the chance for Tony to prove himself to the woman he loves by making every decision about the wedding. He and Pippa have agreed in the presence of a lawyer that she will know absolutely nothing until the big day. Three weeks apart will push their relationship to the limit. Will his best men give him the best advice?

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Warren Boone, the chief people officer of restaurant chain Cocos Bakery, steps off from the corporate ladder to go undercover on the front line.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Mayor of Fontana, Acquanetta Warren, goes undercover in the Californian city to spend time with sewer workers and the fire brigade to see how the city is recovering from Covid.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Austin, Texas, home to struggling restaurant El Greco, a business in danger of imminent collapse. El Greco is owned by mother and son Athina and Jake, and claims to be an authentic Greek restaurant. Much to Gordons disgust, the duo have secretly recruited `Chef Mike the microwave to help them out on a daily basis.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon is in Atlanta, Georgia, to visit Michons, a family-run smokehouse restaurant with a future thats about to go up in smoke because of mismanagement and lazy cooking. Michons owner, Al Wilson, had always hoped that one day he would pass his beloved restaurant over to his daughter, Natalie. But when he suffered a collapsed lung, the handover came about much earlier than expected.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling
programme.desc: With three girls under the age of 10, Kylah and Jon have been too busy to move, but they desperately need a bigger house and the Property Brothers step in with a plan. Yet, when the house hunt hits a snag, Kylah and Jon are faced with a choice.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Birmingham, Talisa and her boyfriend Luke kick off the competition with a traditional Italian menu. They are joined by Kelly and Rob, and retiree Denise and her partner Peter.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: Greg Davies and Alex Horne return as Frankie Boyle, Ivo Graham, Jenny Eclair, Kiell Smith-Bynoe, and Mae Martin unravel some string, throw toilet brushes, and dance around a giant golden pineapple.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Host Jimmy Carr and team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson are joined by celebrity guests Miles Jupp and Rhod Gilbert. Plus, Nick Helm joins Susie Dent in `Dictionary Corner.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr, Sean Lock and Jon Richardson are joined by Kathy Burke and Josh Widdicombe. Susie Dent is in `Dictionary Corner with assistance from Dara O Briain, while Rachel Riley provides the letters and numbers aided by comedian Joe Wilkinson.

programme.title: The IT Crowd
programme.desc: Douglas hires Jen as his new PA, giving Moss and Roy free reign in the office. How will they cope without her, and how will she cope with Douglass unwelcome advances?

programme.title: The IT Crowd
programme.desc: While boss Douglas does his best to run Reynholm Industries into the ground, Jen has to deal with a builder from hell. Meanwhile, Roy loses 20 quid, and Moss learns how to stand up to ruffians.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Extreme
programme.desc: Pash mops up a ghastly gravy gaffe, Sketch chases away a confusing chicken tattoo, Alice gives Danni an inspirational design, and Uzzi deletes a DIY disaster.

programme.desc: Greg Davies and Alex Horne return as Frankie Boyle, Ivo Graham, Jenny Eclair, Kiell Smith-Bynoe, and Mae Martin unravel some string, throw toilet brushes, and dance around a giant golden pineapple.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Host Jimmy Carr and team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson are joined by celebrity guests Miles Jupp and Rhod Gilbert. Plus, Nick Helm joins Susie Dent in `Dictionary Corner.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Loud and proud duo Fay and Wayne kick things off in the Midlands with their comfy country cuisine featuring a beef Wellington main course. Day two sees retired lovebirds Rob and Maz hosting with their take on nouveau cuisine.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Glasgow, tour operator and laird Davey decides to make a confession which leaves his wife Irene and the rest of the guests gobsmacked. Has it scuppered their plans for bagging the grand?

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One in a London Nightclub
programme.desc: Dean wants to relive his clubbing days by organising a nostalgic wedding in a London nightclub. His bride-to-be, Sandra, has been made to wait nearly a decade for her big day and shes desperate for her childhood church followed by a reception in a castle.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Adam lets fate take a hand as he plans to give his fiancee Bianca the wedding of a lifetime. Will the pregnant bride-to-be get her elegant, classic day to remember?

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Ryan wants his and bride-to-be Marias wedding to take place at the college where they first met. Will Maria give Ryan top marks or will he face detention?

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Holly is hoping for a warm, cosy, family wedding, but her cowboy-loving fiance Cole has a Wild West theme in mind.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Khloe is dreaming of a romantic wedding ceremony on a Mediterranean beach, but her fiance Lawrence has his sights set on a funfair-themed day.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Anthony Wedo, president and CEO of buffet restaurant organisation Buffets, Inc., goes undercover posing as a construction worker taking part in a reality TV competition to win enough money to start up his own business.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Amit Kleinberger, CEO and co-owner of frozen yogurt chain Menchies, goes undercover posing as a participant in a reality TV contest who is trying to win money to fund and develop a new business.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon is in Atlanta, Georgia, to visit Michons, a family-run smokehouse restaurant with a future thats about to go up in smoke because of mismanagement and lazy cooking. Michons owner, Al Wilson, had always hoped that one day he would pass his beloved restaurant over to his daughter, Natalie. But when he suffered a collapsed lung, the handover came about much earlier than expected.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Philadelphia to visit a Mexican restaurant called Zocalo. At one point, this restaurant played Cupid to a young couple who met, fell in love and married while working here together. Greg and Mary Russell loved the place so much that they pooled their resources and bought it. However, it seems that Cupids arrow had a poisoned tip: the restaurant has become an albatross around their necks.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to Fremont, California, to try to save a restaurant that is tearing the owners marriage apart. Saki Kavouniaris took over the failing Spin-a-Yarn restaurant in 1995 and began to turn it into a success. He fell in love with a waitress, but the restaurant and the marriage are now failing.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon visits Italian bistro Charlies in La Verne, Southern California, which was bought in 2010 by Tatiana Leiva, her mother and her sister. Tatiana didnt actually put any money into the purchase herself, but her hard-working mother and sister did because they felt they were helping to fulfil Tatianas dream. They even put up their houses as collateral! Unfortunately, soon Charlies was in trouble.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to Baltimore to try to turn around a restaurant that is suffering because its owner, Denise Whiting, has managed to alienate the surrounding community. When Denise opened Cafe Hon in 1992 it was a roaring success, but as a result of her unwavering business ambition the local community decided to boycott the restaurant, leaving Denise frustrated by falling sales.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and Big Zuu take on Danny Dyer and Helen Bauer. Mat Ewins joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.

programme.title: Bill Bailey: Cosmic Jam
programme.desc: In his 1996 debut live stand-up show, Bill Bailey demonstrates the influence that Cockney music has had on the great classical composers, and warns of the perils of a world ruled by insects, while playing the second-biggest gong in Britain.

programme.title: Derry Girls
programme.desc: The gang are beside themselves with excitement as they have tickets to see Take That in Belfast. When Ma Mary forbids them to go they decide that they will stop at nothing to get there.

programme.title: Derry Girls

programme.desc: After learning no one made the deposit on the wedding venue, Geoff scrambles to help fix the oversight before Beverly finds out; and to make matters worse, a major storm is imminent which could jeopardise the entire event.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Elle has her heart set on a classy wedding at Liverpools iconic Liver Building. But Phil plans a big day to go down in history. Will his scheme turn out a wreck?

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: The competitive spirits of certain individuals are ignited when Manny takes up fencing and Phil sets out to find where Lukes talents lie.

programme.title: Modern Family

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: A short clip from Rude Tubes naughty little sister Rude-ish Tube, featuring food and animals.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon heads to Scottsdale, Arizona, where he visits Amys Baking Company and meets husband-and-wife bistro owners Samy and Amy Bouzaglo, who reveal a shocking contempt for customer feedback.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barry and his friends try to reclaim their youth by forming a boy band, but they quickly learn they arent kids anymore; Adam has the keys to Ericas downtown apartment but realises hes not cut out for city life.

programme.title: Alita: Battle Angel
programme.desc: Set several centuries in the future, the abandoned Alita is found in the scrapyard of Iron City by Ido, a compassionate cyber-doctor who takes the unconscious cyborg Alita to his clinic. When Alita awakens, she has no memory of who she is, nor does she have any recognition of the world she finds herself in. As Alita learns to navigate her new life and the treacherous streets of Iron City, Ido tries to shield her from her mysterious past.

programme.title: Arabesque
programme.desc: Ancient language specialist David thinks he has won the jackpot when a mysterious Arab offers him a fortune to translate a Hittite inscription. What he doesnt know is that the words contain a code which could save a Middle Eastern prime minister from assassination.

programme.title: Fantastic Voyage
programme.desc: A surgical task force is reduced to microbe size to perform a delicate operation inside the brain of a dying diplomat.

programme.title: Spartacus
programme.desc: Spartacus, who is living in brutal conditions and is forced to fight to his death for the pleasure of wealthy Romans, leads a heroic struggle for freedom. His army of gladiators is swelled by slaves who pillage estates and are trying to escape from Italy.

programme.title: Trainspotting
programme.desc: Set in an underbelly of Edinburgh the city fathers never dreamed of, this is the story of Mark and his so-called friends - a bunch of losers, liars, psychopaths, thieves and junkies. This tragi-comedy charts the disintegration of their friendships as they proceed, seemingly inevitably, towards self-destruction.

programme.title: James Martins British Adventure
programme.desc: James culinary tour of Britain continues in Scotland with a trip to Blair Castle in the Highlands. He is joined by top Scottish Michelin starred chef Tom Kitchin. Their journey begins with an incredible tour of the castle grounds complete with awe inspiring views. James kicks off the cooking with his venison with beetroot and blackberries in the castles walled garden. Next Tom Kitchin dusts of his wellies and heads in to the woods for a bit of wild mushroom foraging.

programme.title: Rick Steins Seafood Odyssey
programme.desc: Its action down under for seafood king Rick as he travels to Queensland, Australia, where he prepares a spicy squid salad, and smoked bream with a tangy bush tucker chutney.

programme.title: Rick Steins Seafood Odyssey
programme.desc: In this episode, he goes to Chesapeake Bay in the US, cooking traditional oyster dishes.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Its biscuit day and Ree Drummond is baking a fantastic foursome to share with friends and family, with chocolate mint-iced biscuits decorated with swirls of snow white royal icing.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond is reclaiming kitchen scraps and transforming them into star dishes. Peels, stems and bones destined for the trash are instead turned into crispy roasted potato peels, stir fried broccoli stems and an awesome chicken broth.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Cooking for Less
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver takes root vegetables to another level, makes scrumptious sticky BBQ chicken, rattles out oozy cheesy enchiladas and turns things upside down with a pineapple cake, all for under a quid a portion.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its crunch time with Biscuit Week, starting off with a luxurious chocolate coated signature bake, then a snack with family significance for Paul and an eye-catching showstopper.

programme.title: Mary Berry Everyday
programme.desc: Mary shares inspiring everyday dishes full of wholesome goodness from her secret tip for a rich bolognaise to a heart-warming soup. She dons a white suit and tries her hand at bee keeping, collecting fresh honey to make a winning pud.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James and his wingman, top chef Mark Jordan, hop aboard their motorbikes to discover the wonderful island of Guernsey. They sample local speciality Gache cake, a seafood platter fit for a king, and James uses Guernseys wonderful dairy produce to make a delicious creamy rice pudding with chargrilled peaches. Finally, James and Mark set themselves a fishing challenge by attempting to catch the King of the Sea: the mighty turbot.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James discovers the delights of Dorset. He is joined on his trip by his mate and Michelin-starred chef Paul Ainsworth. They hop aboard a millionaires yacht, where James cooks a delicious sea bass dish. Paul discovers some fantastic local cheese, while James visits a breeder with mangalista pigs in a beautiful coastline setting. The highlight of their day is a beachside barbecue, where they feast on some amazing pork and chicken cooked al fresco.

programme.title: Ottolenghis Mediterranean Feast
programme.desc: Yotam Ottolenghis Mediterranean journey concludes in the country where he grew up: Israel. In Tel Aviv, he explores Israels freshest produce, samples seafood with a twist and cooks alongside cutting-edge chefs. Yotam explores the national obsession with hummus and begins by making the popular Mediterranean dish shakshuka, where eggs are gently poached in a spicy sauce of tomatoes, onion and hot chilli.

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Italy
programme.desc: Foodie Adam Richman embarks on a delicious culinary adventure across Italy. He visits the places that are home to the most iconic Italian dishes.

programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieri dives into delicious dishes on both coasts. In Palo Alto, a funky joint puts out fresh California cooking, like spicy pork and knockout gnocchi. In Wilmington, a bakery-restaurant makes major meatloaf.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: On this trip, Guy Fieris digging into a multicultural flavourfest. In Hawaii, a poke place knocking it out of the park with their island specialties. In San Carlos, a crazy Italian spot putting tasty twists on their homemade pasta.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: On this trip, Guy Fieri plays a tasty game of chicken all over the country. In Alameda, a joint cooks chicken Nashville hot-style and puts a gourmet twist on a burger.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.sub-title: On the Hook and in the Bun
programme.desc: Guy Fieri reels in off-the-hook seafood and digs into some serious sandwiches on this trip. In San Francisco, a Chinese emporium cooks up lobster, pork buns and even dim sum chicken feet.

programme.title: Man v. Food Best Of
programme.desc: Pulled pork and a huge cinnamon roll; the Colossal Challenge including a giant sandwich and a 116 oz. milkshake.

programme.title: Man v. Food Best Of
programme.desc: Expert eater Adam Richman chows down on an amazing steak sandwich in Lewiston, New York. Afterwards, he takes on the mammoth challenge of eating a total of 74 whole ounces of steak.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: The boys go golfing to get inspiration for a golf tournament cake and feel `lost in space while creating a cake for a local planetarium.

programme.title: Cake Boss

programme.desc: The bakery heavyweights are challenged to an eating contest and one of Frankies biggest fears is exposed while he is making a complicated cake.

programme.title: Jamies Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: The average price of the recipes in this episode is just £1.96 per portion. Jamie cooks up a roast shoulder of lamb with all the trimmings and turns the leftovers into Moroccan-style crispy pastillas.

programme.title: Jamies Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: Here, the average price of the recipes is just £1.89 per portion. Jamie cooks up glorious pot roasted ham hocks with parsley sauce, and roasts a butternut squash which he turns into a stunning squash and spinach rotolo.

programme.title: James Martin Digs Deep
programme.desc: Jamess fruit trees and strawberries bear enough fruit for a delicious pavlova, one of his favourite puddings.

programme.title: James Martin Digs Deep
programme.desc: James uses his three-week-old herb bed to cook two delicious recipes: salmon with sorrel and peas and lemon verbena creme brulee for dessert.

programme.title: James Martin Digs Deep
programme.desc: James heads to Yorkshire for advice on tomatoes and, after getting in a tangle planting them back home, is over the moon when the first baby tomatoes appear and he can cook a tasty mozzarella and tomato dish.

programme.title: James Martin Digs Deep
programme.desc: James cooks two of his grannys recipes with his homegrown gooseberries and carrots. He then takes off on his motorbike for a charity bike run delivering chocolate to a childrens hospital.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go West
programme.desc: The Bikers journey continues, bringing the boys cruising the roads along the 220-mile River Severn, a region steeped in history and natural beauty.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go West
programme.desc: The Bikers reach Devon and Dorset for the final leg of their tour. In Dorset, they visit a watercress farm and meet a blacksmith, and in Devon, they search for biltong and learn all about the fermentation and distilling of white rum.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Ride Route 66
programme.desc: In small town America, the bikers enjoy some retro roadside spectacles that provide entertainment and intrigue, including the huge fibreglass figures known as `muffler men. Dave and Si then enjoy a championship winning apple pie and learn how Route 66 entered American folklore by connecting small town America from east to west and enabling it to prosper.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: On the second leg of their Route 66 adventure, Dave Myers and Si King set out to discover what lies beneath the legend of this iconic road, a journey which takes them through three states. The bikers also visit a roadside museum described as a love letter to Route 66. The current owner, like her father before her, is passionate about keeping the magic of the fabled road alive.

programme.title: Prue Leiths Cotswold Kitchen

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the grand finale as the winning chefs cook their dishes at a banquet for the heroes of the NHS. Having arrived at St Bartholomews Hospital, the chefs are shown the incredible location for event, the almost three hundred year old Great Hall.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: London and South East - Starter and Fish Courses
programme.desc: Competing for the top spot are 3 new chefs to the competition representing some of the most exciting culinary talent from London, Paul Walsh, Luke Selby and Ben Marks.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: London and South East Mains and Desserts
programme.desc: The three chefs are halfway through their heat and the pressure is mounting. Only two will proceed to Fridays judging and have a chance to represent the London and the South East region at the regional finals.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining London chefs must cook their four course menus again. The chefs need to impress the formidable judges: food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver as well as a guest judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: North East Starter and Fish Courses
programme.desc: Three chefs new to the competition compete in the North East heats. Three of the regions chefs battle it out for the topspot: Mike Carr, Samira Effa, and Tom Anglesea. The three chefs must present the menu of their careers, inspired by 50 years of British pop music.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs are halfway through their heat and will serve up their main courses and desserts. The stakes are high and again they are each serving a unique take on the pop music brief. Mike is confident about his northern dish, `Rack n Roll, which puts both classic Yorkshire hogget and traditional Lancashire hotpot in the spotlight.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North East chefs must cook their four course menus again for the judges: food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver as well as a guest judge, one of the British pop industrys finest.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Newcomer Kray brings an urban edge to proceedings, with a starter inspired by Grime Music called `Fire in the Booth. It combines hot sauce, veal sweetbreads and tuna belly. Returner Sabrina is compiling a dish of her favourite ingredients, including pasta and ox cheek, for her Italian inspired starter called `My Mixtape.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: For his main course, Ryan Simpson-Trotman is confident his dish, `The UKs Number 1, will hit the top spot with his combination of a beef pie and rump cap. Sabrina Gidda is taking inspiration from her grandads collection of old 45s for her `Sunday Jukebox main: pork Wellington and Black Country faggots.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Central chefs must cook their four course menus again for judges food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver as well as a guest judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Competing for a place on the banquet menu are two chefs returning to the competition for a second time, plus a supremely talented newcomer. Liam Simpson, who co-owns a high-end gastropub near Henley-on-Thames, is hoping to make it all the way after being knocked out in the heats during the 2018 competition.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs are halfway through the regional heats and, as the lowest scoring chef will be eliminated from the competition at the end of the show, the pressure is on. They start by cooking their all-important main courses, the centrepiece of the banquet menu. Michelin-starred newcomer Hrishikesh Desai is pushing himself to the limit with a complex multi-part venison dish, inspired by the bands which play at the Kendal Calling music festival.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining chefs from the north west cook their dishes head to head for a panel of expert judges, in a bid to secure themselves a place in the national finals. Will they have acted on the advice given by the veteran judge earlier in the week, and learn from their mistakes?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of Scotlands finest chefs do battle for a chance to cook at the banquet celebrating 50 Years of Popular Music. Gordon Jones, the only newcomer, faces returners Lorna McNee and Ben Reade, who are going head-to-head for the second time.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs cook their mains and desserts, each battling to produce smash hit dishes that wow their veteran. For her main, Lorna is confident her lamb dish `With A Little Help From My Friends, dedicated to supportive friends, is a celebratory dish fit for the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Scottish chefs must cook their four courses again, but this time theyll need to appeal to four new palates: food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton, broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver plus a celebrity guest judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Emily Scott is the chef proprietor of Cornwalls renowned `St Tudy Inn. Her two rivals are from across the sea on Jersey. Joe Baker is largely self-taught, but at just 28 already running his own restaurant, `No 10, in St Helier. A neighbouring restaurant is Michelin starred `Samphire where Lee Smith is head chef.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Emilys David Bowie inspired dish celebrates Britains food heroes and shes using Cornish duck meat from her local producer that she pairs with grilled radicchio and Malbec sauce. Joe has sourced his ingredients locally too, using hay from his family farm and Jersey beef.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs need to impress the esteemed judges: food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver as well as a guest judge, one of the pop stars the banquet will celebrate. They are joined by one half of the synthpop duo The Communards, Reverend Richard Coles.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Tom `Westy Westerland is a former National Chef of Wales and head chef of the Brasserie at Lucknam Park in Wiltshire. Cindy Challoner is the former head chef at prestigious teaching restaurant `The Classroom in Cardiff. They face Andy Sheriden formerly of Sosban restaurant in Llanelli, returning to the competition for his second attempt at making it to the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Andy cooks venison in celebration of his favourite band, Oasis. He accompanies the loin with rhubarb and beetroot ketchup, black pudding puree, pickled beetroot and a pistachio crumb. Westys beef dish `The Cow Shed is named after a former stage at the Glastonbury festival.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining chefs from Wales must cook their four course menus again. They must take on board their veterans advice as they serve their tributes to Britains music success.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three chefs begin by cooking their starters for a surprise veteran of the competition. Chris dish `Brixton Academy Through the Years is his refined take on the classic lamb, post gig junk food he ate after nights out at Brixton Academy.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chris is cooking his interpretation of the classic beef dish Tournedos Rossini using guinea fowl. His inspiration is Proms in the Park and the British artists who mix classical and modern music styles. Alexs dish commemorates his late brother and the song by Northern Ireland artist DonnaTaggart that he associates with the difficult time of his death.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining chefs from Northern Ireland will recreate their four course menu for the esteemed judges: food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver. Theres also a guest judge, one of the pop stars the banquet will celebrate.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs battle to get their starter course to the Abbey Road banquet and dont disappoint with stunning dishes dedicated to everything from Tom Jones to Grime. The rivals are cooking to impress food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton, broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver, plus guest judge and British music legend Martin Kemp.

programme.title: Great British Menu
